title: Christmas Cookie Box or Serving Display
summary: A small shallow tray-box on little feet for serving cookies or displaying holiday treats. Made from furring strips and a plywood bottom in an afternoon.
description:
A shallow open box on four small feet, sized to serve cookies or display holiday treats — the kind of quick, giftable build you can knock out from furring strips and a plywood scrap. A back board holds a painted message or a cut-vinyl phrase, and the whole thing paints up in an evening.

The construction is honest and simple: a 1x3 frame around a 1/4" plywood bottom, with a taller 1x4 or 1x6 back board for lettering, and four little 2x2 feet. The only technique worth flagging is the feet — don't try to cut a 1-1/2" cube off the end of a short offcut with a power saw, because holding a tiny piece near a blade is how people lose fingers. Cut the little feet OFF the end of a longer piece (at least 16") so your hands stay well clear, then trim the block down.

Clamp the plywood to your bench and cut it with a circular saw for a clean, safe cut, glue and brad it into the center of the box, and paint. Add the feet with glue and a pocket screw at each corner for grip.

tools.note: Crosscuts and the plywood bottom (clamped to the bench).

materials.name: 2x2 scrap, 16"+
materials.note: Feet — cut from a long piece, never a tiny offcut.

cutList.note: Cut off the end of a 16"+ piece — do not cut tiny pieces at the saw.

steps.title: Assemble the frame
steps.body: Nail the frame boards together with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue. Use a 1x6 or 1x4 for the back board, which stands taller to carry lettering.

steps.title: Add the bottom
steps.body: Cut the 1/4" plywood bottom with a circular saw, clamping the plywood to your bench for a clean, safe cut. Glue and attach with 3/4" brad nails or staples, fastening into the center of the box.

steps.title: Add the feet and finish
steps.body: Cut the four little feet off the end of a longer (16"+) piece so your hands stay clear of the blade — never cut tiny pieces at the saw. Attach the feet with glue and a 1-1/4" pocket screw at each corner for grip. Paint, and add lettering (painted or cut vinyl) to the back board.

images.alt: Shallow wooden cookie serving box on small feet with a lettered back board
